description: Crimes drawn randomly for the AI judge. Each charge is read via TTS and displayed on the dashboard.

crimes.charge: The defendant stands accused of claiming to have read the terms and conditions.
The defendant stands accused of maintaining 247 open browser tabs with intent to never read them.
The defendant stands accused of clicking 'Remind me tomorrow' on a software update for eleven consecutive months.
The defendant stands accused of typing 'lol' with a completely straight face.
The defendant stands accused of watching a tutorial at 2x speed and claiming mastery of the subject.
The defendant stands accused of unplugging it and plugging it back in, and billing this as technical expertise.
The defendant stands accused of owning a drawer of cables for devices they no longer possess.
The defendant stands accused of replying to a group chat three days late with 'sorry, just saw this.'
The defendant stands accused of using their smart TV's on-screen keyboard without filing for emotional damages.
The defendant stands accused of taking 14 nearly identical photos and deleting none of them.
The defendant stands accused of rating an app 5 stars solely to make the popup go away.
The defendant stands accused of saying 'we should hang out soon' with no intention of following through.
The defendant stands accused of waving at someone who was waving at the person behind them, then committing to the wave.
The defendant stands accused of saying 'you too' when the waiter said 'enjoy your meal.'
The defendant stands accused of pretending not to see an acquaintance in the grocery store.
The defendant stands accused of asking 'how are you' while continuing to walk away.
The defendant stands accused of laughing at a joke they did not hear.
The defendant stands accused of rehearsing an argument in the shower against an opponent who was not present.
The defendant stands accused of saying 'I'm five minutes away' while still at home.
The defendant stands accused of holding the door for someone slightly too far away, forcing them to jog.
The defendant stands accused of forgetting a person's name immediately upon introduction, then knowing them for six years.
The defendant stands accused of clapping when the airplane landed.
The defendant stands accused of telling a long story that had no ending, point, or discernible structure.
The defendant stands accused of eating fries out of the bag before arriving home.
The defendant stands accused of microwaving fish in a shared workplace.
The defendant stands accused of double-dipping at a social gathering while maintaining eye contact.
The defendant stands accused of putting an empty container back in the refrigerator.
The defendant stands accused of claiming they were 'not hungry' and then eating half of someone else's fries.
The defendant stands accused of letting a banana achieve full decomposition on the counter while claiming they would 'make banana bread.'
The defendant stands accused of biting into a chocolate chip cookie that was, in fact, oatmeal raisin, and blaming the cookie.
The defendant stands accused of drinking directly from the carton in the presence of cups.
The defendant stands accused of taking the last slice without performing the customary offer ritual.
The defendant stands accused of owning seventeen spices and using exclusively salt.
The defendant stands accused of operating a chair that functions solely as a clothing repository.
The defendant stands accused of setting seven alarms and respecting none of them.
The defendant stands accused of cleaning their room by relocating one pile to a different location.
The defendant stands accused of saving a glass jar for a purpose that has never materialized.
The defendant stands accused of leaving one square of toilet paper on the roll to avoid replacement duty.
The defendant stands accused of running the dishwasher at 40% capacity in a time of crisis.
The defendant stands accused of stepping over the same item on the stairs for three weeks.
The defendant stands accused of walking suspiciously.
The defendant stands accused of existing in a manner unbecoming.
The defendant stands accused of thinking about crime.
The defendant stands accused of having a face that suggests they did it.
The defendant stands accused of blinking in a courtroom. This courtroom.
The defendant stands accused of standing where they are currently standing.
The defendant stands accused of breathing audibly with malice aforethought.
The defendant stands accused of being dry. The court finds this suspicious.
The defendant stands accused of crimes. You know what you did.
The defendant stands accused of approaching this exhibit voluntarily, which implies guilt.
The defendant stands accused of conspiracy to remain un-squirted.
The defendant stands accused of saying 'I could build that' at a maker faire and building nothing.
The defendant stands accused of collecting free stickers for a laptop that has no remaining surface area.
The defendant stands accused of starting a project with great enthusiasm and abandoning it at 90% completion.
The defendant stands accused of buying a Raspberry Pi with no plan, no purpose, and no remorse.
The defendant stands accused of explaining their hobby to someone who asked a simple yes-or-no question.
The defendant stands accused of owning a 3D printer that exclusively prints upgrades for itself.
The defendant stands accused of saying 'it works on my machine' as a legal defense.
The defendant stands accused of hoarding components for a project scheduled to begin 'someday.'
not giving me that merch shirt I didn't pay for even though I'm a really big fan.
